mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive return frequency drops below 55% during rate decline periods.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending economic contraction, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in > 60% of triggered instances or signal loses predictive power (t-stat < 1.5) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent positive momentum in retail sales and housing starts confirms synchronized consumer spending across durable and non-durable sectors, lifting aggregate corporate revenues and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint signal fails to outperform baseline by > 2% annualized or produces negative returns in > 50% of co-movement periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during periods of high capacity utilization reflects supply-side tightening and pricing power for manufacturers, leading to margin expansion and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses or Rank IC drops below 0.01 when capacity utilization exceeds 85%, or 20-day returns are negative in > 55% of triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly GDP growth rate falling below 1.5 percent signals severe economic deceleration and impending recession, prompting immediate portfolio de-risking and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days following data release.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in > 65% of low-growth quarters or signal shows no statistical significance (p > 0.10) in event-study analysis.

mechanism_narrative: A widening gap between the federal funds rate and core inflation indicates falling real interest rates, which reduces the discount rate applied to long-duration equity cash flows and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Real rate decline coincides with negative 60-day returns in > 50% of observations or interaction term loses explanatory power (F-test p > 0.05) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Nonfarm payrolls increasing by more than 200,000 in a single month confirms robust labor market expansion, supporting consumer spending trajectories and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in > 60% of high-payroll months or signal predictive accuracy (Rank IC) falls below 0.03 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in retail sales during a monetary tightening cycle signals demand destruction from higher borrowing costs, triggering immediate equity sell-offs and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Signal produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances during tightening periods or fails to maintain directional accuracy (t-stat < 1.8) in out-of-sample validation.
